tool_name: Inline Static Mixer — Pressure Drop, Mixing Length & Coefficient of Variation

inputs.name: Process Fluid Dynamic Viscosity (μ)
inputs.note: Water at 20°C = 1.0 mPa·s; light oil ≈ 10; heavy oil ≈ 500–5000 mPa·s

inputs.name: Static Mixer Element Type
inputs.note: K_p = pressure drop factor: ΔP_mixer = K_p × ΔP_empty_pipe; from manufacturer data

inputs.name: Number of Mixer Elements (N_e)
inputs.note: Kenics: 6–12 for turbulent; 20–30 for laminar. Sulzer SMX: 5–8 elements typically

inputs.name: Required Coefficient of Variation at Mixer Exit (CoV_req)

formulas: A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(pipe_ID_mm / 1000, 2); v_m_s = flow_rate_m3h / 3600 / A_pipe_m2; Re = fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_m_s * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 / (fluid_viscosity_mPas / 1000)   // [m/s, dimensionless] Pipe Velocity & Reynolds Number Reference: Re = ρ·v·D/μ — flow regime governs mixing mechanism and ΔP correlation

f_fanning = (Re < 2300) ? 16/Re : 0.0791 * Math.pow(Re, -0.25); L_mixer_m = num_elements * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 * 1.5; dP_pipe_pa = 4 * f_fanning * (L_mixer_m / (pipe_ID_mm/1000)) * fluid_density_kg_m3 * Math.pow(v_m_s, 2) / 2   // [Pa] Empty Pipe Pressure Drop (ΔP_pipe) — Darcy-Weisbach Reference: Fanning friction: laminar f=16/Re; turbulent Blasius f=0.0791Re^−0.25; element length ≈ 1.5D
dP_mixer_pa = mixer_type * dP_pipe_pa   // [Pa] Mixer Total Pressure Drop (ΔP_mixer) Reference: ΔP_mixer = K_p × ΔP_empty_pipe over mixer length — Sulzer / Chemineer product data

CoV_0 = 1.0; CoV_actual = (Re > 10000) ? CoV_0 * Math.pow(0.316, num_elements) : CoV_0 * Math.pow(0.5, num_elements * 0.7)   // [dimensionless] Coefficient of Variation After N Elements (CoV_actual) Reference: Turbulent: CoV reduces by factor ~0.316 per element (Kenics HEV); laminar: slower reduction; Perry's §18.5

decay_factor = (Re > 10000) ? 0.316 : 0.5 ** 0.7; N_req = Math.ceil(Math.log(CoV_required) / Math.log(decay_factor))   // [count] Minimum Elements Required for Target CoV Reference: N_req = log(CoV_req / CoV_0) / log(decay_factor) — Kenics design method

L_total_m = num_elements * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 * 1.5; epsilon_w_kg = dP_mixer_pa * v_m_s / fluid_density_kg_m3   // [m, W/kg] Total Mixer Length & Specific Energy Dissipation (ε) Reference: ε = ΔP·v/ρ — specific energy input; turbulent mixing requires ε > 1 W/kg for fast reactions per AIChE Mixing Fundamentals

standards: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

V1.error_msg: Actual CoV exceeds required mixing quality: add more elements (N_req) or switch to higher-performance mixer type (e.g. SMX from Kenics)

V2.error_msg: Very low Reynolds number (Re < 100): laminar segregation — Kenics HEV/SMXL insufficient for viscous mixing; use Sulzer SMX designed for Re < 10

V3.error_msg: Mixer pressure drop > 5 bar: very high ΔP — consider low-pressure SMXL elements, larger pipe diameter, or reduce flow velocity

smart_warnings.message: Laminar flow with Kenics HEV: designed for turbulent use — switch to Sulzer SMX (K_p=37) optimised for viscous laminar mixing
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: Element count below minimum for target CoV — mixing quality not achieved; add N_req − N_e elements or accept higher CoV
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: High velocity in dense fluid: erosion risk on mixer elements — verify element material hardness; consider ceramic-coated elements for slurries
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: Low specific energy dissipation (<0.1 W/kg): mixing is gentle — suitable for shear-sensitive products (emulsions, cells); otherwise increase velocity
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

smart_warnings.message: High viscosity fluid (>10 Pa·s): static mixer will have very high ΔP — evaluate dynamic (agitated) mixer or reduce viscosity by heating before mixing
smart_warnings.source: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide
